mission.title: Install and Configure Etcd on Kubernetes
mission.description: etcd is a distributed reliable key-value store designed for critical data in distributed systems. It is widely used for its simplicity, security, speed, and reliability, making it an essential component for many applications, including Kubernetes.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Bitnami Helm repository to your local Helm configuration to access the etcd chart.
```bash
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install etcd using Helm
steps.description:
Install etcd with the specified version in a new namespace called 'etcd'.
```bash
helm install etcd bitnami/etcd --version 12.0.18 --namespace etcd --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the etcd pods to ensure they are running.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace etcd
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the etcd deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment etcd --namespace etcd --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for etcdctl
steps.description:
Forward the etcd port to access it locally for testing and management.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/etcd --namespace etcd 2379:2379
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the etcd Helm release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall etcd --namespace etcd
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any custom resource definitions and remaining resources related to etcd.
```bash
kubectl delete crd etcds.etcd.database.coreos.com
kubectl delete namespace etcd
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Backup the current etcd data before upgrading.
```bash
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot save snapshot.db --endpoints=http://localhost:2379
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure the Helm repository is up to date before upgrading.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade etcd
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the etcd release to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade etcd bitnami/etcd --version 12.0.18 --namespace etcd
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the etcd pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace etcd
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the etcd pods are stuck in the Pending state, check for insufficient resources in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace etcd
```

troubleshooting.title: etcdctl unable to connect
troubleshooting.description:
If etcdctl cannot connect, ensure that the port forwarding is set up correctly and that the service is running.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace etcd
```

troubleshooting.title: High latency in etcd operations
troubleshooting.description:
If you experience high latency, check the resource usage of the etcd pods and consider scaling up resources.
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace etcd
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of etcd will have the etcd pods running in the 'etcd' namespace, and you will be able to connect to it using etcdctl. Resource limits will be configured to ensure optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm package manager installed.
